data.title: $2.7 Trillion Vanishes: Wall Street Just Had Its Wildest Reversal Since April
data.description:
Wall Street walked into Thursday expecting a clean rebound—what it got instead was the most jarring intraday reversal since April, a swing that turned early optimism into one of the sharpest sentiment resets in months. What began as a market cheering Nvidia's (NVDA, Financial) upbeat revenue outlook and Walmart's (WMT, Financial) steady consumer pulse quickly morphed into something far more chaotic. Traders spent the afternoon trying to piece together what triggered a near-5% swing in the Nasdaq-100: a delayed but strong September jobs report that hinted the Federal Reserve may not cut rates again this year, Bitcoin sliding to a six-month low, and renewed debate on whether the AI spending boom is generating enough near-term payoff to justify the scale of investment.

By the close, over $2.7 trillion in equity value had evaporated. The S&P 500 (SPY, Financial), up as much as 1.9% earlier, finished down 1.6%—its weakest level since mid-September—while the Nasdaq-100 dropped 2.4%, widening its pullback from its late-October record to 7.9%. Volatility gauges spiked: the VIX pushed above 26 for the first time since April, and tech's VXN jumped past 32 ahead of Friday's expiration of roughly $3.1 trillion in options. Mega-caps were tossed around with rare force, with Tesla (TSLA), Alphabet, Apple, Microsoft, Broadcom and Amazon all seeing swings exceeding $100 billion in market value. Nvidia, the early star of the session, became the biggest drag, flipping a 2.4% gain into a 3.2% drop that erased nearly $400 billion from its intraday high as worries about the durability of AI-chip spending resurfaced.

The damage ran even deeper across the market's riskier edges. The most-shorted stocks fell 3.5%, profitless tech slid 3.7%, and the Russell Microcap Index widened its decline from a record to 10%. Analysts framed the selloff as a market confronting a long list of unresolved debates—labor strength, tariffs, inflation, private-credit strains, crypto's retreat, and whether AI investments today could become meaningfully profitable in the coming years. Some suggested early gains were simply short-covering, while others pointed to systematic funds sitting in fragile positioning that could be forced sellers on a deeper pullback. As Piper Sandler's Craig Johnson noted, investors may need more time for market breadth to stabilize, and until then, the questions overshadowing this market could keep traders cautious about chasing a year-end rally.

data.title: Archer (ACHR) and Joby (JOBY) Stocks Plunge Amid Heated Legal Clash
data.description:
Archer Aviation (ACHR, Financial) and Joby Aviation (JOBY, Financial) moved lower on Thursday after Joby sued Archer, alleging the rival gained access to confidential files through a former employee.

Joby slipped about 5%, and Archer fell roughly 8% amid broader market weakness and investor caution.

In its complaint filed in California, Joby said the ex-employee downloaded internal documents shortly before leaving and later joined Archer.

Joby also alleges one of its partners was approached with knowledge of private deal terms, and it described the conduct as "corporate espionage."

Archer rejected the claims. The company's chief legal officer told CNBC the complaint "has no merit" and said Joby did not identify a specific trade secret or show misuse.

A court hearing is scheduled for March 20, 2026, meaning the dispute may remain in focus for investors and analysts.

Shares may continue to fluctuate as legal documents, testimony and discovery unfold.

data.title: Veolia (VEOEY) to Acquire Clean Earth for $3 Billion
data.description:
Key Takeaways:

  • Veolia Environnement (VEOEY, Financial) plans to acquire Clean Earth for $3 billion, enhancing its U.S. hazardous waste operations.
  • The acquisition is expected to generate $120 million in synergies by the fourth year and increase earnings per share from the second year.
  • Veolia has raised its EBITDA growth target to at least 10% from 2024 to 2027.

Veolia Environnement (VEOEY) has announced its strategic move to acquire U.S.-based Clean Earth from Enviri (NVRI) for a substantial $3 billion. This acquisition, slated for completion by mid-2026, is poised to significantly bolster Veolia's presence in the U.S. hazardous waste sector. The transaction is anticipated to deliver $120 million in synergies by the fourth year, with earnings per share accretion expected from the second year. This expansion will effectively double Veolia's U.S. footprint, driving revenues to €5.2 billion with a robust 17% EBITDA margin. Additionally, Veolia has revised its EBITDA growth target to a minimum of 10% annually from 2024 to 2027.

Veolia Environnement SA, the world's largest water company, is a prominent player in the waste management sector, with significant operations in France, the United Kingdom, Germany, the United States, and Australia. The company also has a strong foothold in energy services, particularly in Central Europe. Veolia's strategic refocus since 2011, including the integration of its historical rival Suez in 2022, has resulted in a substantial 40% increase in EBITDA.

With a market capitalization of €23.6 billion, Veolia operates within the Industrials sector, specifically in the Waste Management industry. The company's current stock price is not mentioned in the news, but it is important to note that Veolia trades on the OTCPK exchange.

Financial Health Analysis

Veolia's financial performance is characterized by consistent revenue growth, with a 3-year revenue growth rate of 9.3%. The company's EBITDA margin stands at 13.92%, while its net margin is 2.48%. These figures indicate a stable profitability profile, supported by an operating margin of 7.59%.

On the balance sheet front, Veolia's current ratio of 0.88 and quick ratio of 0.83 suggest a need for improved liquidity management. The company's debt-to-equity ratio is 1.96, indicating a relatively high level of leverage. The Altman Z-Score of 0.85 places Veolia in the distress zone, highlighting potential financial vulnerabilities.

Valuation & Market Sentiment

Veolia's valuation metrics reveal a P/E ratio of 21.41, which is close to its 3-year low, suggesting potential undervaluation. The P/S ratio of 0.46 and P/B ratio of 1.95 are also noteworthy. Analyst targets indicate a target price of €21.48, with a recommendation score of 2.3, reflecting a moderate buy sentiment.

Technical indicators such as the RSI of 33.74 suggest that the stock is approaching oversold territory. Institutional ownership is relatively low at 6%, with no significant insider activity reported in the past year.

Risk Assessment

Veolia's financial health grades indicate areas of concern, particularly in terms of liquidity and leverage. The Altman Z-Score highlights potential financial distress, while the debt-to-equity ratio suggests a high level of financial risk. Sector-specific risks include regulatory changes and environmental compliance costs.

Veolia's beta of 0.56 indicates lower volatility compared to the market, providing some stability. However, investors should remain vigilant regarding upcoming catalysts and potential market shifts.

data.title: Alibaba Q2 Earnings Preview: What To Expect From Upcoming Report
data.description:
Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba (BABA, Financial) is set to release its Q2 FY26 results on Tuesday. The stock has climbed more than 90% year-to-date, supported by strong e-commerce performance and rising demand for cloud and AI services.

In Q1 FY26, Alibaba reported revenue of 247.7 billion yuan ($34.6 billion), while cloud sales rose 26% to 33.4 billion yuan. Analysts expect Q2 earnings of $0.85 per share, down from $2.10 a year earlier, with revenue projected at $34.29 billion, a year-over-year increase of roughly 4%.

Investors will be closely watching whether gains in cloud computing, AI, and emerging consumer-AI products can offset pressures in Alibaba's core e-commerce business, which faces soft consumer spending, discounting, and intense competition in China.

Is BABA Stock a Buy?

Based on the one year price targets offered by 39 analysts, the average target price for Alibaba Group Holding Ltd is $198.19 with a high estimate of $271.11 and a low estimate of $119.85. The average target implies a upside of +24.73% from the current price of $158.89.

Based on GuruFocus estimates, the estimated GF Value for Alibaba Group Holding Ltd in one year is $115.14, suggesting a downside of -27.53% from the current price of $158.89. gf value is gurufocus' estimate of the fair value that the stock should be traded at. it is calculated based on the historical multiples the stock has traded at previously, as well as past business growth and the future estimates of the business' performance. For deeper insights, visit the forecast page.

data.title: Nvidia Stock Pops After Q3 Beat -- Data Center Growth Explodes 66%
data.description:
Shares of Nvidia (NVDA, Financial) rose about 5% on early Thursday trading following fiscal third-quarter results that topped Wall Street forecasts.

The semiconductor company reported revenue of $57 billion, surpassing analysts' $54.9 billion estimate. Adjusted earnings per share reached $1.30, above the $1.25 consensus. Net income jumped 65% to $31.9 billion from $19.3 billion a year ago.

CEO Jensen Huang said demand for the company's Blackwell GPUs is “off the charts.” Nvidia expects roughly $65 billion in revenue for the current quarter, above the $61.7 billion analysts had projected.

Data center sales continue to drive growth, climbing to $51.2 billion, a 66% year-over-year increase. Of this, $43 billion came from compute-focused GPUs, while networking products added $8.2 billion.

Gaming, professional visualization, and automotive/robotics units also posted gains. DGX Spark AI desktops and Blackwell Ultra chips contributed to growth.

Nvidia noted limited sales in China due to geopolitical and competitive constraints. CFO Colette Kress confirmed $12.5 billion in share repurchases and $243 million in dividends during the quarter.

data.title: Why Tesla Trillion Price Tag Looks More Like a Bet on Elon Than the Business
data.description:
Tesla's valuation remains the central issue for investors as the company navigates mixed business signals and high market expectations.

The electric-vehicle maker trades at multiples that put it well above legacy automakers. At roughly a $1.34 trillion market capitalization, Tesla's sales multiple of 14x sits many times higher than peers Ford (F, Financial) and General Motors (GM, Financial), which trade at fraction-of-sales levels. That gap has puzzled some analysts and investors.

Automotive revenue still drives the bulk of Tesla's business, but growth has slowed from earlier in the decade. Its energy unit shows stronger growth but remains small relative to the stock's price tag. The expectation that either segment will close the valuation gap has not materialized.

Speculation about robotaxis and full self-driving (FSD) often fuels optimistic price targets. Tesla has run pilots and showcased concepts such as a Cybercab, yet commercial robotaxi revenues remain negligible. Independent competitors, notably Waymo, already offer paid, driverless rides in limited markets, underscoring the challenges ahead.

People continue discussing the AI-related products of Tesla and Optimus robots. Optimus has yet to receive any money, and the FSD packages are not yet mainstream items, yet a small number of buyers choose a paid version. Those facts make it difficult to jump to the thought that new lines will help to bring in big money somewhere.

There are two things that investors must balance: what would happen to the company in case Tesla abandons its ambitious plans, and the danger of the sky-high expectations having already been priced in.

To the portfolio managers, Tesla is more of a bet on Elon and the hype than on the numbers.
